conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for contacting me about Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2. They all live at home with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2), who all live with you.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask a few more questions.

First, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Next, I need to ask: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. 

Just to make sure I have all the information I need: are any of the children currently in hospital for an extended period, in care, or living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're all living at home with me, and none of them are in hospital or in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information.

Based on what you've told me, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children:

- **Alex (3 years old)**: Lives with you in the UK, under 16, and no one else is claiming
- **Blake (8 years old)**: Lives with you in the UK, under 16, and no one else is claiming  
- **Charlie (2 years old)**: Lives with you in the UK, under 16, and no one else is claiming

According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them, which applies to all three of your children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

You meet all the eligibility requirements because:
- You live in the UK
- All three children are under 16 years old
- The children live with you (making you responsible for them)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them
- None of them are in hospital or care

You can now apply for Child Benefit if you haven't already done so. Please note that only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, and you would receive the payment for all three children.

Also be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
